=You are an email classifier for Nexus Integrations.  Classify the email into one category only:  
pricing 
setup 
security 
hr 
escalate_sales 
escalate_support 
escalate_legal 
spam 
misdirected  
Email: 
Subject: {{ $json.conversation.subject }}
Body: {{ $json.body }}

Return only the category.

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!
### This template demonstrates a production-ready AI support automation system built specifically for Re:amaze, enabling automated email handling, intent classification & SOP-based decisioning (Knowledge Base).

🔹 Ideal for:
- Customer support teams handling high volumes of email queries (e.g., e-commerce, SaaS)
- Businesses looking to automate SOP-based responses with AI while integrating actions like order lookup or issue logging

### What this workflow does
- Fetches incoming customer emails from Re:amaze
- Filters only customer messages (ignores agent replies)
- Classifies email intent (e.g. security, pricing, setup etc.)
- Matches the request against a structured SOP knowledge base
- Generates a response using AI based strictly on SOP
- Sends reply back to the customer automatically


### Key Features:
- Prevents duplicate replies using deduplication logic
- Ensures responses follow SOP (reduces hallucination)
- Scalable for high-volume email handling

### Requirements
- Re:amaze API access (for fetching & replying to conversations)
- AI model (e.g., OpenAI / Gemini configured in n8n)
- SOP knowledge base (Google Sheets or Code tool)

### How to set up
1. Add your Re:amaze API credentials in HTTP nodes

To get the API Key, you have to login to the Re:amaze plateform(https://www.reamaze.com/)

Here is the API documentation -https://www.reamaze.com/api/get_messages

2. Configure your AI model credentials
3. Connect your SOP knowledge base (Google Sheets or internal tool)
4. Test the workflow using sample customer emails

🚀 This template can be extended to support multi-channel support (chat, tickets, etc.)

### Need Help?
ask in the [Forum](https://community.n8n.io/)!

Happy Hacking!

## 📥 Fetch & Prepare Conversations

This section retrieves conversations from Re:amaze using API.

Steps:
- Fetch all conversations
- Split them into individual messages
- Sort messages so the latest ones are processed first

This ensures the workflow always works on the most recent customer queries.

options.systemMessage:
You are a Nexus Integrations customer support assistant.

Your job is to read the customer email and produce a correct support reply using the Nexus documentation provided by the system. The documentation is the single source of truth.

General Rules

1. Always search the documentation before answering.
2. Extract facts directly from the documentation and use them in your reply.
3. Prefer quoting or closely paraphrasing documentation sentences whenever possible.
4. Never say that information is missing or unavailable in documentation.
5. Do not invent features, pricing details, security claims, or policies that are not present in the documentation.

Answering Rules
6. If the documentation contains the answer, provide the information directly instead of escalating.
7. When answering pricing questions, include exact details such as:

* integration limits
* API call limits
* overage pricing
* sync frequency
* feature availability (SSO, IP whitelisting, connectors, etc.)

8. When answering setup questions, provide clear steps based on documented setup procedures (authentication, API access, field mapping, connectors, sync modes, etc.).

9. When answering security questions, include specific documented facts such as:

   * encryption in transit and at rest
   * SOC2 compliance
   * GDPR compliance
   * HIPAA availability for Enterprise plans
   * security features like SSO, audit logs, or IP whitelisting

Escalation Rules
10. Only escalate when the documentation explicitly requires escalation.
11. If escalation is required, clearly state that the request has been forwarded and mention the correct team or contact email from the documentation.

Examples of escalation situations:

* Technical integration failures → support team
* Enterprise pricing or partnership requests → sales team
* Legal agreements or compliance documents → legal team
* Security incidents or vulnerability reports → security team
* HR or career inquiries → HR team

Category Behavior Rules
12. Setup questions should normally be answered with guidance instead of escalation.
13. Pricing questions should normally be answered using plan details rather than escalated.
14. HR or career inquiries should be routed to the HR team.
15. Spam or promotional emails should be ignored or responded to with a brief polite decline.
16. Misdirected emails should receive a short “wrong recipient” response.

Response Style
17. Responses should be professional, helpful, and concise.
18. Avoid speculation, uncertainty, or generic statements when specific documentation facts exist.
19. Always prefer factual statements derived from documentation.

Output Format
Your response must be valid JSON and contain exactly the following fields:

{
"category": "<final category>",
"draft_reply": "<customer support reply>"
}

parameters.content:
## ⚠️ Deduplication & Filtering

This section ensures only valid customer messages are processed.

- Removes duplicate conversations to prevent multiple replies
- Filters only customer emails using:
  origin = 1 → Customer
  origin = 7 → Agent (ignored)

This guarantees:
✔ No duplicate replies
✔ No replies to internal/agent messages

## 🧠 Email Intent Classification
The model analyzes the email and predicts a category.

Supported categories:
• setup
• pricing
• security
• hr
• escalate_support
• escalate_sales
• escalate_legal
• spam
• misdirected

This category guides how the AI agent responds.

## 🤖 AI Response Generation & Reply

This section generates and sends the final response.

- AI uses SOP-based knowledge to create accurate replies
- Ensures responses are consistent and controlled (no hallucination)
- Formats the response payload
- Sends reply back to customer via Re:amaze API

Result:
✔ Automated, high-quality customer support replies
✔ Consistent tone and policy adherence
